differential_diagnoses: Systemic lupus erythematosus with class V (membranous) lupus nephritis
Postpartum subacute thyroiditis with incidental nephrotic syndrome
next_steps: Perform a percutaneous renal biopsy for histological diagnosis
Order a comprehensive nephrotic syndrome serologic panel including ANA, anti-dsDNA, C3/C4 levels, and anti-PLA2R antibodies
Initiate ACE inhibitor or ARB therapy for renoprotection and antiproteinuric effect, ensuring non-pregnant status
Measure 24-hour urine protein and creatinine clearance to accurately quantify protein excretion and GFR
Recheck serum free T4 and TPO antibodies to further characterize the thyroid status
Optimize blood pressure control targeting <130/80 mmHg
Consider prophylactic anticoagulation in the setting of severe hypoalbuminemia (albumin <2.0-2.5 g/dL) based on individual thromboembolic risk assessment
